zh.lifeSummary: 當Mesceline or Sibilla Braci於大約1163年出生在英國史洛普爾時，她的父親Aldulfus de Braci28歲，母親Agnes de Brus17歲。她於1177年與Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe在英國巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波洛結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1238年3月8日在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波洛享年76歲，埋葬在英國肯特艾勒斯福德艾勒斯福德 普利奧利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mesceline or Sibilla Braci于大约1163年出生在英国史洛普爾时，她的父亲Aldulfus de Braci28岁，母亲Agnes de Brus17岁。她于1177年与Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe在英国巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波洛结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1238年3月8日在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波洛享年76岁，埋葬在英国肯特艾勒斯福德艾勒斯福德 普利奧利。

nameMeanings.name: Braci
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Cheskel, Moshe, Chaim, Mendel, Pinchas, Bernat, Hershel, Meyer, Moche, Mort, Rivka, Rivky.

German: topographic name from Middle High German brache ‘fallow land, pastureland’, originally ‘newly plowed land’. Alternatively, a habitational name from any of the places called Braach (Hesse) or Brach (Baden-Württemberg, Franconia).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Barach  .

variants.name: Breach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Donal.
English (Sussex): variant of   Brach  .

variants.name: Brecher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Chaim, Yosef, Baila, Chana, Ephraim, Herschel, Hershel, Meir, Meyer, Pnina, Shimon.  German Armin, Oskar, Bernd, Erwin, Gerhard.
English: variant of Brach 2, from Middle English breche, Old English brǣc, brēc ‘newly cultivated land’, the -er suffix denoting an inhabitant.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from an agent derivative of German brechen ‘to break’, an occupational name for someone who crushed hemp or flax, or a nickname for a law breaker or evildoer.

variants.name: Bracher
meanings: English: from an agent derivative of Middle English brech ‘land broken up for cultivation’ (Old English brǣc, brēc), denoting a dweller by newly cultivated land. Compare Brach 2 and Breach .
Swiss German: variant of German Brachmann (see Brachman ).
German: variant of   Brach   1.

variants.name: Brachman
meanings: Americanized form of German Brachmann: variant of Brach , with the addition of Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Brech
meanings: German: variant of   Brach   1 or   Brecht  .
English: variant of   Breach   (see   Brach   2).

variants.name: Barach
meanings: Americanized form of Croatian and Serbian Barać and Barač (see Barac ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): acronymic surname from the first letters of the Hebrew patronymic phrase Ben Rabi Hayyim ‘son of rabbi Hayyim’.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

nameMeanings.name: Sibilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybille
meanings: From Greek. See Sybil .

variants.name: Sibella
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Mesceline or Sibilla Braci was born about 1163, in Shropshire, England, her father, Aldulfus de Braci, was 28 and her mother, Agnes de Brus, was 17. She married Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe in 1177, in Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 8 March 1238, in Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76, and was buried in Aylesford Priory, Aylesford, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mesceline or Sibilla Braci est née environ 1163, à Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Aldulfus de Braci, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Brus, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe en 1177, à Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 mars 1238, à Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterrée à Aylesford Priory, Aylesford, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mesceline or Sibilla Braci um 1163 in Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Aldulfus de Braci 28 und ihre Mutter Agnes de Brus 17. Sie hat 1177 in Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, England Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. März 1238 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Aylesford Priory, Aylesford, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mesceline or Sibilla Braci è nata il circa 1163 nel Shropshire, England, sua padre, Aldulfus de Braci, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Agnes de Brus, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe nel 1177, nel Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 8 marzo 1238, nel Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolta a Aylesford Priory, Aylesford, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Mesceline or Sibilla Braciが約1163年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Aldulfus de Braciは28歳，母Agnes de Brusは17歳であった。1177年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアエレスボロウでSir William Warwicksire de Cantilupeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1238年3月8日にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアエレスボロウで76歳で死去し，イギリスケントエイレスフォードエイレスフォード プリオリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mesceline or Sibilla Braci(이)가 대략 1163년 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Aldulfus de Braci(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Brus(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1177년 영국 벅잉함실르 엘레스버루에서 Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1238년 3월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 엘레스버루에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 아일레스퍼드 아일레스퍼드 프리오리에 묻혔다.

mn.of Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, Englan: Ellesborough, Buccingahamscir, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mesceline or Sibilla Braci nasceu aproximadamente 1163, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Aldulfus de Braci, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Brus, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe em 1177, em Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de março de 1238, em Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 76 anos, e foi sepultada em Aylesford Priory, Aylesford, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mesceline or Sibilla Braci родилась около 1163 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Aldulfus de Braci было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Brus, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe в 1177 г. в местности эллесбороую, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 8 марта 1238 г. в местности эллесбороую, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности аилесфорд приори, аилесфорд, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mesceline or Sibilla Braci nació aproximadamente 1163, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Aldulfus de Braci, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Agnes de Brus, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Sir William Warwicksire de Cantilupe en 1177, en Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 8 de marzo de 1238, en Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultada en Aylesford Priory, Aylesford, Kent, Inglaterra.
